title: Problems & Repairs
goal: Report problems and talk about simple repairs on site.

completionCriteria.summary: Finish the quiz with at least 80%.

vocabulary.example: There is a leak in the pipe.
I see a crack in the wall.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: We have a problem with the pipe.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: What is wrong? Is there a leak?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: Yes, there is a leak. Water is on the floor.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: No, we must replace the pipe.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: Okay. This bolt is loose too.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: Please make it tight with the wrench.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: The drill is broken. I cannot use it.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: I see a crack in the wall as well.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: Let us tell the foreman about the problem.

multipleChoice.prompt: The machine does not work. It is ___.
multipleChoice.explanation: Broken means it does not work.

multipleChoice.prompt: Water comes out of the pipe. There is a ___.
multipleChoice.explanation: A leak is water coming out.

multipleChoice.prompt: The bolt moves and is not tight. It is ___.
multipleChoice.explanation: Loose is the opposite of tight.

multipleChoice.prompt: The old pipe is bad. We must ___ it.
multipleChoice.explanation: Replace means put a new one.

multipleChoice.prompt: I see a line in the wall. It is a ___.
multipleChoice.explanation: A crack is a line or break in the wall.

fillBlank.prompt: The drill does not work. It is ___.
Use the wrench to make the bolt ___.
fillBlank.explanation: Tight is the opposite of loose.

matching.instruction: Match the word to its meaning.
Match the word to its meaning.
